content_blocks.raw_text: - Identify emotions in a book, picture, or on someone's face - Describe themselves (interests, family, looks, gender) - Show positive self-identity - Display pride in their accomplishments - Ask for support from adults when needed

- Begin to recognize their own emotions before reacting - Use words and respectful language to express thoughts and emotions - Increasingly use a variety of strategies to self-soothe - Attempt to complete tasks independently - Demonstrate confidence in meeting their own needs - Seek new challenges with familiar materials and activities

- Describe a range of individual characteristics and interests and communicate how these are similar or different from other people - Begin to recognize the emotions of peers and respond with empathy and compassion - Recognize appropriate reactions to social situations - Show care and concern for others - Express gratitude for someone or something in their life - Communicate how other children or adults may be feeling and why

- Maintain friendships over time - Demonstrate strategies for participating in social play with peers - Express feelings and needs, including in conflict situations - Engage in prosocial behavior such as cooperating, compromising, turn-taking, and resolving social conflict - Seek and accept help when needed - Use problem-solving strategies independently or with adult prompting when feeling angry or frustrated

- Show eagerness to learn about a variety of topics - Consider ideas from adults and other children in finding a solution or strategy - Seek and accept help when needed - Use problem-solving strategies independently or with adult prompting - Identify people in the community who can help them (e.g., firefighter, nurse) - Learn and follow basic personal health and safety rules
